summary: Tested Blayake landing site backend (FastAPI/Mongo) and frontend (React single-page). 7/7 backend pytest tests passed (health, valid+invalid lead creation, persistence, ordering, no _id leakage). Frontend renders all expected sections and data-testid elements; CTAs anchor-scroll correctly; FAQ accordion works (faq-toggle-0 open-by-default and collapses on click; faq-toggle-1 expands); contact form end-to-end POST /api/leads returns 201 and shows success ('Got it...') and clears fields; scroll-progress, marquee track, footer wordmark + ©2026 all present; no console errors.

integration_issues.issue: Submitting 'notanemail' does NOT round-trip to backend / does not surface a 422 error in form-status. The email input is type='email' so the browser's native HTML5 validator blocks submission before axios fires (form-status remains empty). Test spec expected an axios 422 error message. This is acceptable UX (native validation), but if the intent was server-side error surfacing in form-status, change input type to 'text' or add custom validation. Not a functional regression.

action_items: (Optional) Decide on invalid-email behavior: either keep native HTML5 blocking or change input type to 'text' so the axios call hits backend and form-status shows the 422 error as the spec implies.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is referenced inside create_lead (line 88) but logger is defined AFTER include_router/middleware (line 116). If the except branch ever runs at module-import time it would NameError; at request-time it's fine since module is fully loaded. Still, move logging.basicConfig + logger = getLogger above the routes for clarity/safety.
server.py: @app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in modern FastAPI — prefer lifespan context manager.
Blayake.jsx is 775 lines — consider splitting into per-section components (Hero, Services, FAQ, Contact, Footer) for maintainability (>700 line guideline).
Lead listing endpoint has no pagination beyond ?limit; fine for MVP but add skip/cursor for production.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created 2 TEST_-prefixed leads (TEST_alice@example.com, TEST_eve@example.com) plus a test_e2e@example.com lead via UI. All persisted in MongoDB leads collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_blayake_api.py — re-run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Frontend public URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All sections use data-testid; FAQ uses aria-expanded on faq-toggle-{n} (no faq-answer-{n} testid — verify via aria-expanded). Contact form input email is type='email' so HTML5 validator blocks invalid submissions before axios fires; this is the only spec-vs-impl mismatch.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Only one spec-implementation mismatch: invalid-email flow doesn't reach backend because input type='email' triggers HTML5 validation before axios call. Mitigation (if desired): change to type='text' or add custom validation that calls API and surfaces 422 in form-status.
